name: DEVS Atomic Model Structure
description: Every DEVS atomic model inherits from src.devs.Atomic and must override four methods: delta_internal() for internal state transitions, delta_external(inputs, elapsed_time) for external input handling, output() to produce messages before internal transitions, and time_advance() returning time until next internal event. Models go passive by returning float('inf') from time_advance().

name: DEVS Output Buffer Pattern
description: Models accumulate messages in internal lists during delta_internal() and delta_external(), then in output() they deepcopy the lists into the result dict keyed by port, and clear the internal lists. This ensures output messages are independent copies and buffers are ready for the next cycle.

name: DEVS Simulation Wiring
description: Simulation setup uses AtomicGraph: add() models, then connect(output_id, output_port, input_id, input_port) for each coupling. The simulation loop calls graph.min_next_time(simulator.time) and simulator.simulate_step() until min_next_time returns infinity. Simulator handles imminent models (internal transition), models with input (external transition), and confluence (both) cases.

content: DEVS models use @override decorator (Python 3.12+) on every overridden method. Debug logging uses print() with tagged prefixes: [INTERNAL], [INPUT], [OUTPUT] in every transition and output function for traceability.

name: DEVS Framework Module Map
description: Core framework files: src/devs/Atomic.py (base class with abstract methods + deal contracts), src/devs/AtomicGraph.py (coupled model graph with connections and routing), src/devs/Simulator.py (simulation engine with step-based execution), src/devs/Types.py (Port = Tuple[int, Type], Id = str), src/devs/IdGenerator.py (unique ID generation with prefix:counter format).

content: Ports are defined as class-level tuple constants with pattern (index: int, type: class). Input ports use set_inport() and output ports use set_outport() in __init__. Port indices are per-direction (input and output indices are independent). The type in the tuple must match between connected output and input ports. See src/devs/Types.py and src/devs/AtomicGraph.py.

All messages exchanged between DEVS models are @dataclass instances. Every dataclass has an 'id' field of type str generated via generateId(prefix) from src.devs.IdGenerator. Union type aliases are used to group related message types (e.g., Order = Union[BuyOrder, SellOrder]).
